item.description: The U.S. military hit two locations in Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, according to a U.S. official. NBC News’ Matt Bradley explains what this means for the war with Iran.

media:content.media:title: U.S. strikes two locations in Iran near Strait of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: The U.S. military hit two locations in Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, according to a U.S. official. NBC News’ Matt Bradley explains what this means for the war with Iran.

item.title: U.S. strikes two locations in Iran near Strait of Hormuz

item.description: The Justice Department is investigating a series of oil market trades that right before important moves related to the war with Iran. At least four trades worth $2.6 billion are being examined. NBC News’ Allie Canal explains the timing of the bets.

media:content.media:title: Source: DOJ investigating suspicious oil trades timed around Iran war
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department is investigating a series of oil market trades that right before important moves related to the war with Iran. At least four trades worth $2.6 billion are being examined. NBC News’ Allie Canal explains the timing of the bets.

item.title: Source: DOJ investigating suspicious oil trades timed around Iran war

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: The U.S. is urging the government to break with Tehran-backed militia groups following more than 600 attacks against U.S. diplomatic facilities.

media:content.media:text: An Iraqi army armored humvee vehicle is deployed to protect the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad
media:content.media:description: An Iraqi army armored vehicle is deployed near the banks of the Tigris River to protect the U.S. Embassy fortified "Green Zone" in Baghdad on March 14.

media:content.media:text: A fire burns outside the grounds of the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad
media:content.media:description: An Iraqi army armored vehicle is deployed near the banks of the Tigris River to protect the U.S. Embassy fortified "Green Zone" in Baghdad on March 14.

item.title: Iran-backed groups targeted U.S. facilities in Iraq 600 times, U.S. official says

item.description: As the Tennessee governor signs a voting map that splits up the state’s only majority Black district, some leaders are calling out the change. NBC News’ Kathy Park explains what lead to the redistricting and responses from both sides of the aisle.

media:content.media:title: Black leaders in Tennessee compare redistricting push to Jim Crow-era voter suppression tactics
media:content.media:description: As the Tennessee governor signs a voting map that splits up the state’s only majority Black district, some leaders are calling out the change. NBC News’ Kathy Park explains what lead to the redistricting and responses from both sides of the aisle.

item.title: Black leaders in Tennessee compare redistricting push to Jim Crow-era voter suppression tactics

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
Natasha Korecki
item.description: Amid turmoil over the party’s future, Kamala Harris has been privately sounding out confidants about her own prospects.

media:content.media:description: Kamala Harris, former Vice President and 2024 Presidential candidate, arrives during the National Action Network (NAN) Convention in New York on April 10, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Kamala Harris Book Tour In Los Angeles
media:content.media:description: Harris spoke in Los Angeles in September for her book tour.

media:content.media:text: California Gov. Newsom Attends A "Yes On Prop 50" Volunteer Event In L.A.
media:content.media:description: Harris spoke at a "Yes on Prop 50" volunteer event at the Los Angeles Convention Center in November.

item.title: Kamala Harris wants the DNC to release its autopsy report on the 2024 campaign

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking voter registration list data from all 50 states and the District of Columbia over the past year.

media:content.media:text: Close up on a sheet of "I Voted" stickers being cut on a table
media:content.media:description: While many states have complied, the Justice Department has sued 30 states and Washington, D.C., for refusing.

item.title: Tracking the DOJ's effort to get U.S. voter registration data

item.description: Five states - Texas, Georgia, Virginia, Arizona, and California - confirm passengers who had left the cruise ship before the Hantavirus outbreak was known about, are back home. None are showing symptoms. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports on what steps are being taken to mitigate the risk of any spread of the virus.

media:content.media:title: Multiple passengers from cruise ship back in U.S.
media:content.media:description: Five states - Texas, Georgia, Virginia, Arizona, and California - confirm passengers who had left the cruise ship before the Hantavirus outbreak was known about, are back home. None are showing symptoms. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports on what steps are being taken to mitigate the risk of any spread of the virus.

item.title: Multiple passengers from cruise ship back in U.S.

item.description: NBC News' Dr. Akshay Syal breaks down everything you need to know about the hantavirus outbreak that has claimed three lives so far.

media:content.media:title: What we know about the hantavirus outbreak so far
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Dr. Akshay Syal breaks down everything you need to know about the hantavirus outbreak that has claimed three lives so far.

item.title: What we know about the hantavirus outbreak so far

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
item.description: Olivia Andre, 19, has been held at the Dilley immigration facility in Texas for nearly two months after her family was released. A judge said she must be freed.

media:content.media:text: Olivia, a 19-year-old nursing student who traveled to Maine with her family from the Democratic Republic of Congo, has been held in Dilley for the past five months.
media:content.media:description: Olivia Andre, a 19-year-old nursing student who traveled to Maine with her family from Congo, has been held in Dilley for the past six months.

media:content.media:text: Estafania Andre, who holds a photo of her sister Olivia, shouted Olivia's name every day while she was in detention at Dilley in hope of locating her.
media:content.media:description: Olivia Andre, a 19-year-old nursing student who traveled to Maine with her family from Congo, has been held in Dilley for the past six months.

media:content.media:text: Carine Mbizi and her son, Joel Andre, and daughter Estafania Andre fled violence in Congo to resettle in Maine.
media:content.media:description: Olivia Andre, a 19-year-old nursing student who traveled to Maine with her family from Congo, has been held in Dilley for the past six months.

media:content.media:text: A woman in a light blue hooded sweatshirt gazes out a window while seated at a wooden table.
media:content.media:description: Olivia Andre, a 19-year-old nursing student who traveled to Maine with her family from Congo, has been held in Dilley for the past six months.

item.title: Teen says judge’s order for her release from immigration detention had her family screaming with joy

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: Daniel Savala, who preached for decades in an Assemblies of God-run college ministry, was sentenced to 30 years in prison without the possibility of parole.

media:content.media:text: Savala poses with Eli Stewart and other young followers in Alaska in 2002.
media:content.media:description: Savala poses with Eli Stewart and other young followers in Alaska in 2002.

media:content.media:text: Joseph Cleveland stands outside the courthouse before delivering his victim impact statement.
media:content.media:description: "You thought yourself invincible," Joseph Cleveland told Daniel Savala while giving an impact statement in court Thursday.

media:content.media:text: Joseph Cleveland as a young man; Daniel Savala's house in 2018.
media:content.media:description: Joseph Cleveland as a teen; Daniel Savala's house in 2018.
Ron Bloomingkemper Jr., a former Chi Alpha member who for years raised alarm about Savala's actions, attended his plea hearing.

media:content.media:text: From left, Krys Scroggins, Joseph Cleveland, Danny Clark, and far right, Ron Bloomingkemper, listen as Daniel Savala is sentenced.
media:content.media:description: Former Chi Alpha members, including Joseph Cleveland and Ron Bloomingkemper, listen as Daniel Savala is sentenced.

media:content.media:text: Joseph Cleveland is embraced after the hearing where he gave an impact statement.
media:content.media:description: Joseph Cleveland hugs a victim support specialist from the prosecutor's office after Savala's guilty plea.

item.title: Christian missionary hailed as ‘holiest man alive’ pleads guilty to abusing boys in Texas

item.description: Blake Lively is asking "It Ends With Us" co-star Justin Baldoni to pay for damages and legal fees not long after a federal judge signed off on a settlement between the two in their long-running legal dispute. NBC News' Chloe Melas has more on the case.

media:content.media:title: Blake Lively asking Justin Baldoni to pay legal fees after settlement
media:content.media:description: Blake Lively is asking "It Ends With Us" co-star Justin Baldoni to pay for damages and legal fees not long after a federal judge signed off on a settlement between the two in their long-running legal dispute. NBC News' Chloe Melas has more on the case.

item.title: Blake Lively asking Justin Baldoni to pay legal fees after settlement

media:content.media:description: At least 400 homes damaged as tornadoes hit Mississippi

item.description: During a visit to inspect the new renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, President Trump was asked about his focus on construction projects in Washington D.C. during an ongoing war with Iran.

media:content.media:title: Trump asked about focus on renovations during war in Iran
media:content.media:description: During a visit to inspect the new renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, President Trump was asked about his focus on construction projects in Washington D.C. during an ongoing war with Iran.

item.title: Trump asked about focus on renovations during war in Iran

item.description: President Donald Trump tells reporters that he's been briefed on the hantavirus and that it's "very much, we hope, under control."

media:content.media:title: Trump says he's been briefed on hantavirus
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump tells reporters that he's been briefed on the hantavirus and that it's "very much, we hope, under control."

item.title: Trump says he's been briefed on hantavirus

item.description: NBC News' Raf Sanchez joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the latest hantavirus outbreak and what the World Health Organization is saying about the possibility of the virus becoming a large-scale risk.

media:content.media:title: Could hantavirus be the next Covid? Experts weigh in.
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Raf Sanchez joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the latest hantavirus outbreak and what the World Health Organization is saying about the possibility of the virus becoming a large-scale risk.

item.title: Could hantavirus be the next Covid? Experts weigh in.
